```{r}
for (i in 1:nrow(best_models)) {
  row <- best_models[i, ]  # Extract the current model row

  # Define hierarchical SEM structure
  mod1 <- lm(as.formula(paste(row$precip, "~ waterYear + Season")), data = sem_data)
  mod2 <- lm(as.formula(paste(row$depth, "~", row$precip)), data = sem_data)
  mod3 <- lm(as.formula(paste(row$stic, "~", row$precip)), data = sem_data)
  mod4 <- lm(as.formula(paste(row$network, "~", row$precip)), data = sem_data)
  mod5 <- lm(as.formula(paste(row$delta, "~", row$precip)), data = sem_data)
  mod6 <- lm(as.formula(paste(row$response, "~", 
                              paste(row$depth, row$stic, row$network, row$delta, sep = " + "))), data = sem_data)

  # Build hierarchical piecewise SEM
  sem_model <- psem(mod1, mod2, mod3, mod4, mod5, mod6)

  # Store model in a list
  fitted_models[[paste(row$response, i, sep = "_")]] <- sem_model
}
data(keeley)

mod <- psem(
  lm(rich ~ cover, data=keeley),
  lm(cover ~ firesev, data=keeley),
  lm(firesev ~ age, data=keeley),
  data = keeley
)

plot(mod)
t <- fitted_models[["DOSat.exc_1"]]
plot(t)
# Plot the best models
for (model_name in names(fitted_models)) {
  print(paste("Plotting SEM for:", model_name))

  plot(fitted_models[[model_name]])
}

coefs <- coefs(t)
print(coefs)

# Convert coefficients into an edge list for igraph
edges <- data.frame(
  from = coefs$Predictor,
  to = coefs$Response,
  weight = coefs$Estimate
)

# Create graph object
g <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = TRUE)

# Plot SEM structure
plot(g, vertex.size = 20, edge.arrow.size = 0.5, main = "SEM Path Diagram")
```

# Graveyard

## LM

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(parallel)

# Combine all predictor groups into a list
predictor_groups <- list(
  flow = flow_vars,
  precip = precip_vars,
  depth = depth_vars,
  stic = stic_vars,
  network = network_vars,
  delta = delta_vars
)

# Add "waterYear" and "Season" as fixed predictors
fixed_predictors <- c("waterYear", "Season")

# Generate all valid combinations of predictors (one from each group)
generate_combinations <- function(predictor_groups) {
  # Create a grid of all possible combinations
  combinations <- expand.grid(predictor_groups, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  return(combinations)
}

# Generate all combinations
predictor_combinations <- generate_combinations(predictor_groups)

# Add fixed predictors to each combination
predictor_combinations <- predictor_combinations |>
  mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) |>
  unite("predictors", everything(), sep = "+") |>
  mutate(predictors = paste0(predictors, "+", paste(fixed_predictors, collapse = "+"))) |>
  pull(predictors)

# Center and scale numeric variables, set waterYear and Season as factors
lmData <- dailyDat |>
  group_by(Site) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ (. - mean(., na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(., na.rm = TRUE), .names = "{.col}")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(waterYear = as.factor(waterYear),
         Season = as.factor(Season)) |>
  drop_na()


# Function to fit a model for a given response and predictor combination
fit_model <- function(response, predictors, data) {
  formula <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", predictors))
  model <- lm(formula, data = data)
  return(model)
}

# Function to evaluate models for a given response variable
evaluate_models <- function(response, predictor_combinations, data) {
  results <- map_dfr(predictor_combinations, ~ {
    model <- fit_model(response, .x, data)
    aic <- AIC(model)
    tibble(
      Response = response,
      Predictors = .x,
      AIC = aic,
      Formula = format(formula(model))
    )
  })
  return(results)
}

# Parallelize the evaluation of models
parallel_evaluate_models <- function(response, predictor_combinations, data, num_cores) {
  # Split the predictor combinations into chunks for parallel processing
  chunks <- split(predictor_combinations, cut(seq_along(predictor_combinations), num_cores, labels = FALSE))
  
  # Create a cluster
  cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
  
  # Export necessary variables and functions to the cluster
  clusterExport(cl, c("fit_model", "evaluate_models", "response", "lmData", "predictor_combinations"))
  
  # Load the `purrr` package on each worker node
  clusterEvalQ(cl, library(purrr))
  clusterEvalQ(cl, library(dplyr))
  clusterEvalQ(cl, library(tidyr))
  
  # Run the evaluation in parallel
  results <- parLapply(cl, chunks, function(chunk) {
    evaluate_models(response, chunk, data)
  })
  
  # Stop the cluster
  stopCluster(cl)
  
  # Combine results from all chunks
  combined_results <- bind_rows(results)
  return(combined_results)
}

# Number of cores to use
num_cores <- detectCores() - 1

# Run the evaluation for all response variables in parallel
all_results <- map_dfr(DOvars, ~ parallel_evaluate_models(.x, predictor_combinations, lmData, num_cores))

# Find the best model for each response variable (lowest AIC)
best_models <- all_results |>
  group_by(Response) |>
  slice_min(AIC, n = 1) |>  # Get the top 10 models for each response
  ungroup() |> 
  distinct(Response, Predictors, AIC)

# Print the best models
print(best_models)

# Refit the best models and calculate R^2
best_models_fitted <- best_models |>
  rowwise() |>
  mutate(
    # Refit the model
    model = list(lm(as.formula(paste(Response, "~", Predictors)), data = lmData)),
    # Extract R^2
    R2 = summary(model)$r.squared,
    # Extract adjusted R^2
    adj_R2 = summary(model)$adj.r.squared
  ) |>
  ungroup()

# Print the best models with R^2 and adjusted R^2
print(best_models_fitted |> select(Response, Predictors, AIC, R2, adj_R2))
# Example: Residuals vs. Fitted plot using ggplot2
for (i in seq_along(best_models_fitted$Response)) {
  response <- best_models_fitted$Response[i]
  model <- best_models_fitted$model[[i]]
  
  # Create a data frame of residuals and fitted values
  plot_data <- data.frame(
    Fitted = fitted(model),
    Residuals = residuals(model)
  )
  
  # Plot using ggplot2
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Fitted, y = Residuals)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
    labs(title = paste("Residuals vs. Fitted for", response),
         x = "Fitted Values", y = "Residuals")
  
  print(p)
}
```

```{r}

# Create a matrix of response variables
response_matrix <- as.matrix(lmData[, DOvars])

# Fit the multivariate linear model
multivariate_model <- lm(response_matrix ~ ., data = lmData[, all_predictors])

# Summarize the model
summary(multivariate_model)

# Extract coefficients
coefficients <- coef(multivariate_model)
print(coefficients)

# Extract R-squared for each response
r_squared <- sapply(summary(multivariate_model), function(x) x$r.squared)
print(r_squared)

# Diagnostic plots for each response variable
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
for (i in seq_along(DOvars)) {
  plot(multivariate_model, which = 1, main = paste("Residuals vs. Fitted for", DOvars[i]))
  plot(multivariate_model, which = 2, main = paste("Q-Q Plot for", DOvars[i]))
  plot(multivariate_model, which = 3, main = paste("Scale-Location for", DOvars[i]))
  plot(multivariate_model, which = 5, main = paste("Residuals vs. Leverage for", DOvars[i]))
}
```

```{r}
# Generate all valid combinations of predictors (one from each group)
generate_combinations <- function(predictor_groups) {
  # Create a grid of all possible combinations
  combinations <- expand.grid(predictor_groups, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  return(combinations)
}

# Generate all combinations
predictor_combinations <- generate_combinations(predictor_groups)

# Add fixed predictors to each combination
predictor_combinations <- predictor_combinations |>
  mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) |>
  unite("predictors", everything(), sep = "+") |>
  mutate(predictors = paste0(predictors, "+", paste(fixed_predictors, collapse = "+"))) |>
  pull(predictors)

# Center and scale numeric variables, set waterYear and Season as factors
lmData <- dailyDat |>
  group_by(Site) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ (. - mean(., na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(., na.rm = TRUE), .names = "{.col}")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(waterYear = as.factor(waterYear),
         Season = as.factor(Season)) |>
  drop_na()

# Function to fit a multiresponse linear model
fit_multiresponse_lm <- function(response_vars, predictors, data) {
  # Create a formula for the multiresponse model
  formula <- as.formula(paste("cbind(", paste(response_vars, collapse = ","), ") ~", predictors))
  
  # Fit the model
  model <- lm(formula, data = data)
  return(model)
}

# Function to calculate multivariate R^2
calculate_multivariate_r2 <- function(model) {
  # Total sum of squares (TSS)
  response_matrix <- model$model[[1]]
  mean_response <- colMeans(response_matrix)
  TSS <- sum((response_matrix - mean_response)^2)
  
  # Residual sum of squares (RSS)
  residuals <- residuals(model)
  RSS <- sum(residuals^2)
  
  # Multivariate R^2
  r2 <- 1 - (RSS / TSS)
  return(r2)
}

# Function to evaluate multiresponse models
evaluate_multiresponse_models <- function(response_vars, predictor_combinations, data) {
  results <- map_dfr(predictor_combinations, ~ {
    model <- fit_multiresponse_lm(response_vars, .x, data)
    
    # Calculate multivariate R^2
    r2 <- calculate_multivariate_r2(model)
    
    # Return results as a tibble
    tibble(
      Predictors = .x,
      R2 = r2,
      Formula = format(formula(model))
    )
  })
  return(results)
}

# Evaluate all multiresponse models
multiresponse_results <- evaluate_multiresponse_models(DOvars, predictor_combinations, lmData)

# Find the best model (highest R^2)
best_model <- multiresponse_results |>
  slice_max(R2, n = 1)

# Print the best model
print(best_model)

# Refit the best model for diagnostics
best_model_fit <- fit_multiresponse_lm(DOvars, best_model$Predictors, lmData)

# Summary of the best model
summary(best_model_fit)

# Extract residuals and fitted values for diagnostic plots
residuals <- residuals(best_model_fit)
fitted_values <- fitted(best_model_fit)

# Combine residuals and fitted values into a data frame
plot_data <- data.frame(
  Fitted = as.vector(fitted_values),
  Residuals = as.vector(residuals),
  Response = rep(DOvars, each = nrow(lmData))
)

# Diagnostic plots using ggplot2
# Residuals vs. Fitted
p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Fitted, y = Residuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs. Fitted", x = "Fitted Values", y = "Residuals")

# Normal Q-Q plot
p2 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(sample = Residuals)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Normal Q-Q Plot", x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles")

# Scale-Location plot
p3 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Fitted, y = sqrt(abs(Residuals)))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Scale-Location Plot", x = "Fitted Values", y = "sqrt(|Residuals|)")

# Print the plots
print(p1)
print(p2)
print(p3)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)

# Response Variables
DOvars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "DOSat.exc")

# Predictor Variable Groups
flow_vars <- c("minQ", "maxQ", "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb")
precip_vars <- c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")
depth_vars <- c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
stic_vars <- c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")
network_vars <- c("ICSL", "IntactStreamLength", "alpha_cent")
delta_vars <- c("dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d")

# Combine all predictor groups into a list
predictor_groups <- list(
  flow = flow_vars,
  precip = precip_vars,
  depth = depth_vars,
  stic = stic_vars,
  network = network_vars,
  delta = delta_vars
)

# Add "waterYear" and "Season" as fixed predictors
fixed_predictors <- c("waterYear", "Season")

# Generate all valid combinations of predictors (one from each group)
generate_combinations <- function(predictor_groups) {
  # Create a grid of all possible combinations
  combinations <- expand.grid(predictor_groups, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  return(combinations)
}

# Generate all combinations
predictor_combinations <- generate_combinations(predictor_groups)

# Add fixed predictors to each combination
predictor_combinations <- predictor_combinations |>
  mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) |>
  unite("predictors", everything(), sep = "+") |>
  mutate(predictors = paste0(predictors, "+", paste(fixed_predictors, collapse = "+"))) |>
  pull(predictors)

# Center and scale numeric variables, set waterYear and Season as factors
lmData <- dailyDat |>
  group_by(Site) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ (. - mean(., na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(., na.rm = TRUE), .names = "{.col}")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(waterYear = as.factor(waterYear),
         Season = as.factor(Season)) |>
  drop_na()

# Function to fit a multiresponse linear model with interactions
fit_multiresponse_lm <- function(response_vars, predictors, data) {
  # Create a formula for the multiresponse model with interactions
  formula <- as.formula(paste("cbind(", paste(response_vars, collapse = ","), ") ~", "(", predictors, ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model
  model <- lm(formula, data = data)
  return(model)
}

# Function to calculate multivariate R^2
calculate_multivariate_r2 <- function(model) {
  # Total sum of squares (TSS)
  response_matrix <- model$model[[1]]
  mean_response <- colMeans(response_matrix)
  TSS <- sum((response_matrix - mean_response)^2)
  
  # Residual sum of squares (RSS)
  residuals <- residuals(model)
  RSS <- sum(residuals^2)
  
  # Multivariate R^2
  r2 <- 1 - (RSS / TSS)
  return(r2)
}

# Function to evaluate multiresponse models
evaluate_multiresponse_models <- function(response_vars, predictor_combinations, data) {
  results <- map_dfr(predictor_combinations, ~ {
    model <- fit_multiresponse_lm(response_vars, .x, data)
    
    # Calculate multivariate R^2
    r2 <- calculate_multivariate_r2(model)
    
    # Return results as a tibble
    tibble(
      Predictors = .x,
      R2 = r2,
      Formula = format(formula(model))
    )
  })
  return(results)
}

# Evaluate all multiresponse models
multiresponse_results <- evaluate_multiresponse_models(DOvars, predictor_combinations, lmData)

# Find the best model (highest R^2)
best_model <- multiresponse_results |>
  slice_max(R2, n = 1)

# Print the best model
print(best_model)

# Refit the best model for diagnostics
best_model_fit <- fit_multiresponse_lm(DOvars, best_model$Predictors, lmData)

# Summary of the best model
summary(best_model_fit)

# Extract residuals and fitted values for diagnostic plots
residuals <- residuals(best_model_fit)
fitted_values <- fitted(best_model_fit)

# Combine residuals and fitted values into a data frame
plot_data <- data.frame(
  Fitted = as.vector(fitted_values),
  Residuals = as.vector(residuals),
  Response = rep(DOvars, each = nrow(lmData))
)

# Diagnostic plots using ggplot2
# Residuals vs. Fitted
p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Fitted, y = Residuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs. Fitted", x = "Fitted Values", y = "Residuals")

# Normal Q-Q plot
p2 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(sample = Residuals)) +
  stat_qq() +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Normal Q-Q Plot", x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles")

# Scale-Location plot
p3 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Fitted, y = sqrt(abs(Residuals)))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~ Response, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Scale-Location Plot", x = "Fitted Values", y = "sqrt(|Residuals|)")

# Print the plots
print(p1)
print(p2)
print(p3)
```

## GAM

```{r}
library(mgcv)
library(MuMIn)  # For model selection
library(future)
library(doParallel)

# Center and Scale, set waterYear and Season as factors
gamData <- dailyDat |> 
  group_by(Site) |> 
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ (. - mean(., na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(., na.rm = TRUE), .names = "{.col}")) |>
    ungroup() |> 
  mutate(waterYear = as.factor(waterYear),
         Season = as.factor(Season)) |> 
  drop_na()

# Setup parallel processing
cl <- makeCluster(detectCores() - 1)  # Use available CPU cores

# Load necessary packages on all worker nodes
clusterEvalQ(cl, {
  library(mgcv)
  library(MuMIn)
})

# Export dataset and necessary objects to workers
clusterExport(cl, varlist = c("gamData"))

# Define the full model formula
full_model_formula <- minDO.pctsat ~ s(waterYear, bs = "re") +
  s(minQ) + s(Q_1d) + s(Q_7d) + s(Q_14d) + s(Q_30d) + s(Q_60d) + s(excProb) +
  s(PPT_1d) + s(PPT_7d) + s(PPT_14d) + s(PPT_30d) + s(PPT_60d) +
  s(Depth_1d) + s(Depth_7d) + s(Depth_14d) + s(Depth_30d) + s(Depth_60d) +
  s(PercWet_1d) + s(PercWet_7d) + s(PercWet_14d) + s(PercWet_30d) + s(PercWet_60d) +
  s(ICSL) + s(IntactStreamLength) + s(alpha_cent) +
  s(dPercWet_7d) + s(dPercWet_14d) + s(dPercWet_30d) + s(dPercWet_60d)

# Fit the full model
full_model <- bam(full_model_formula, data = gamData, na.action = na.fail, select = TRUE, cluster = cl)

# Parallelized dredge using pdredge
model_selection <- dredge(full_model, trace = TRUE)

# Stop cluster after computation
stopCluster(cl)


# Select the best model based on AICc
best_model <- get.models(model_selection, 1)[[1]]

# Summary of the best model
summary(best_model)

# Display the best models based on AIC (ΔAIC < 2)
top_models <- get.models(model_set, subset = delta < 2)
print(top_models)
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(mgcv)  # For GAMs
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)
library(progressr)  # For progress bar

# Response Variables
DOvars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "DOSat.exc")

# Predictor Variable Groups
flow_vars <- c("minQ", "maxQ", "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb")
precip_vars <- c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")
depth_vars <- c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
stic_vars <- c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")
network_vars <- c("ICSL", "IntactStreamLength", "alpha_cent")
delta_vars <- c("dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d")

# Combine all predictor groups into a list
predictor_groups <- list(
  flow = flow_vars,
  precip = precip_vars,
  depth = depth_vars,
  stic = stic_vars,
  network = network_vars,
  delta = delta_vars
)

# Add "waterYear" and "Season" as fixed predictors
fixed_predictors <- c("waterYear", "Season")

# Generate all valid combinations of predictors (one from each group)
generate_combinations <- function(predictor_groups) {
  combinations <- expand.grid(predictor_groups, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  return(combinations)
}

# Generate all combinations
predictor_combinations <- generate_combinations(predictor_groups)

# Center and scale numeric variables, set waterYear and Season as factors
lmData <- dailyDat |>
  group_by(Site) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ (. - mean(., na.rm = TRUE)) / sd(., na.rm = TRUE), .names = "{.col}")) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(waterYear = as.factor(waterYear),
         Season = as.factor(Season)) |>
  drop_na()

# Function to fit GAMs with smoothing for a given response variable and predictor combination
fit_gam <- function(response, predictors, data) {
  # Add smoothing terms to all numeric predictors
  smooth_terms <- sapply(predictors, function(pred) {
    if (is.numeric(data[[pred]])) {
      return(paste0("s(", pred, ")"))
    } else {
      return(pred)  # Keep non-numeric predictors as-is
    }
  })
  
  # Create the formula
  formula <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", paste(smooth_terms, collapse = "+")))
  
  # Fit the GAM model
  bam(formula, data = data, method = "fREML", select = TRUE, na.action = na.fail)
}

# Set up parallel backend
num_cores <- 5
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Initialize a list to store results
results <- list()

# Enable progress bar
handlers("progress")     # Use the "progress" handler

# Parallelized loop over response variables with progress bar
with_progress({
  p <- progressor(along = DOvars)  # Initialize progressor
  
  results <- foreach(response = DOvars, .packages = c("dplyr", "mgcv", "progressr"), .combine = "c") %dopar% {
    response_results <- list()
    
    # Loop through each predictor combination
    for (i in 1:nrow(predictor_combinations)) {
      predictors <- c(fixed_predictors, unlist(predictor_combinations[i, ]))
      
      # Fit the full GAM model with smoothing
      gam_model <- fit_gam(response, predictors, lmData)
      
      # Extract model performance metrics (AIC and adjusted R-squared)
      aic_value <- AIC(gam_model)
      adj_r_squared <- summary(gam_model)$r.sq
      
      # Store the results
      response_results[[paste(response, i, sep = "_")]] <- list(
        response = response,
        predictors = predictors,
        full_model = gam_model,
        AIC = aic_value,
        adj_r_squared = adj_r_squared
      )
    }
    
    # Update progress bar
    p(sprintf("Completed %s", response))
    
    response_results
  }
})

# Stop the parallel backend
stopCluster(cl)

# Function to find the best model for each response variable
find_best_models <- function(results, metric = "AIC") {
  best_models <- list()
  
  for (response in DOvars) {
    # Filter results for the current response variable
    response_models <- results[grep(paste0("^", response, "_"), names(results))]
    
    # Extract the specified metric (AIC or adj_r_squared)
    if (metric == "AIC") {
      best_model <- response_models[[which.min(sapply(response_models, function(x) x$AIC))]]
    } else if (metric == "adj_r_squared") {
      best_model <- response_models[[which.max(sapply(response_models, function(x) x$adj_r_squared))]]
    } else {
      stop("Invalid metric. Use 'AIC' or 'adj_r_squared'.")
    }
    
    # Store the best model for the current response variable
    best_models[[response]] <- best_model
  }
  
  return(best_models)
}

# Find the best models for each response variable using AIC
best_models_aic <- find_best_models(results, metric = "AIC")

# Find the best models for each response variable using adjusted R-squared
best_models_adj_r_squared <- find_best_models(results, metric = "adj_r_squared")

# Print the best models
print(best_models_aic)
print(best_models_adj_r_squared)
```

## SEM

```{r}
# Scale the data
dailyDat_scaled <- dailyDat |> 
 mutate(across(
    where(is.numeric) & !ends_with("_scaled"),
    ~ scale(.x) |> as.vector()
  )) |> 
  as.data.frame()

# Define variable groups
flow_vars <- c("minQ", "maxQ", "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "excProb")
precip_vars <- c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")
depth_vars <- c("minDepth_scaled", "maxDepth_scaled", "Depth_1d_scaled", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
stic_vars <- c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d", "ICSL", "IntactStreamLength", "alpha_cent")
delta_vars <- c("dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d")

# Generate all possible _day variable selections
flow_combos <- flow_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", flow_vars)]
precip_combos <- precip_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", precip_vars)]
depth_combos <- depth_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", depth_vars)]
stic_combos <- stic_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", stic_vars)]
delta_combos <- delta_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", delta_vars)]

# Get all possible combinations of one variable per group
day_combinations <- expand.grid(flow_combos, precip_combos, depth_combos, stic_combos, delta_combos, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Function to create the model based on a given combination of _day variables
create_model <- function(flow_var, precip_var, depth_var, stic_var, delta_var) {
  glue::glue('

    # Structural Model
    avgDO.pctsat ~ {flow_var} + {depth_var} + {stic_var} + excProb
    
    {flow_var} ~ {precip_var} + {depth_var}
    {stic_var} ~ {precip_var} + {depth_var}
  ')
}

# Set up parallel processing
plan(multisession)

# Fit models in parallel
model_results <- day_combinations |> 
  future_pmap(function(Var1, Var2, Var3, Var4, Var5) {
    model_str <- create_model(Var1, Var2, Var3, Var4, Var5)
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      sem(model_str, data = dailyDat_scaled), #, control = list(optimizer = "nlminb")
      error = function(e) return(NULL)  # Return NULL if model fails
    )
    
    if (!is.null(fit)) {
      list(model = model_str, fit = fit)  # Store fit object directly
    } else {
      NULL
    }
  }, .progress = TRUE,
  .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)) 
#beep(sound = 8)
plan(sequential)

# 1. Filter out the failed models
good_models <- model_results[!sapply(model_results, is.null)]

# 2. Extract fit indices from each model.
#    We extract AIC, BIC, RMSEA, CFI, TLI, and SRMR.
fit_indices_list <- lapply(good_models, function(x) {
  indices <- lavaan::fitMeasures(x$fit, c("aic", "bic", "rmsea", "cfi", "tli", "srmr"))
  # Combine the model string with its fit indices
  c(model = x$model, indices)
})

# Convert the list to a data frame
fit_indices_df <- do.call(rbind, fit_indices_list) |>
  as.data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Convert numeric columns from character to numeric
numeric_cols <- c("aic", "bic", "rmsea", "cfi", "tli", "srmr")
fit_indices_df[numeric_cols] <- lapply(fit_indices_df[numeric_cols], as.numeric)

# 3. Rank the models by AIC (lowest AIC is considered better)
best_models <- fit_indices_df[order(fit_indices_df$aic), ]

# View the top 10 best models based on AIC
head(best_models, 10)

top_n <- 10
# Extract the indices for the top N best models
best_model_indices <- as.numeric(rownames(best_models))[1:top_n]

# Loop through the selected models and plot each
for(i in best_model_indices) {
  semPaths(good_models[[i]]$fit, what = "std", layout = "tree", edge.label.cex = 1.2)
}

mod <- modindices(good_models[[120]]$fit)
```

```{r}
# Scale the data (if needed, scale within groups)
dailyDat_scaled <- dailyDat |>
  group_by(Site) |>  # Replace 'group' with your grouping variable (e.g., site, subject)
  mutate(across(
    where(is.numeric) & !ends_with("_scaled"),
    ~ scale(.x) |> as.vector()
  )) |>
  ungroup() |>
  as.data.frame()

# Define variable groups
flow_vars <- c("minQ", "maxQ", "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", )
precip_vars <- c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d")
depth_vars <- c("minDepth_scaled", "maxDepth_scaled", "Depth_1d_scaled", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
stic_vars <- c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d")
delta_vars <- c("dPercWet_7d", "dPercWet_14d", "dPercWet_30d", "dPercWet_60d")

# Generate all possible _day variable selections
flow_combos <- flow_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", flow_vars)]
precip_combos <- precip_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", precip_vars)]
depth_combos <- depth_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", depth_vars)]
stic_combos <- stic_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", stic_vars)]
delta_combos <- delta_vars[grepl("_\\d+d", delta_vars)]

# Get all possible combinations of one variable per group
day_combinations <- expand.grid(flow_combos, precip_combos, depth_combos, stic_combos, delta_combos, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Function to create the multilevel SEM model based on a given combination of _day variables
create_multilevel_model <- function(flow_var, precip_var, depth_var, stic_var, delta_var) {
  glue::glue('
    # Structural Model
    avgDO.pctsat ~ {flow_var} + {depth_var} + {stic_var} + excProb
    
    {flow_var} ~ {precip_var} + {depth_var}
    {stic_var} ~ {precip_var} + {depth_var}
  ')
}

# Set up parallel processing
plan(multisession)

# Fit multilevel models in parallel
model_results <- day_combinations |>
  future_pmap(function(Var1, Var2, Var3, Var4, Var5) {
    model_str <- create_multilevel_model(Var1, Var2, Var3, Var4, Var5)
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      sem(model_str, data = dailyDat_scaled, cluster = "Site"),  # Replace 'group' with your grouping variable
      error = function(e) return(NULL)  # Return NULL if model fails
    )
    
    if (!is.null(fit)) {
      list(model = model_str, fit = fit)  # Store fit object directly
    } else {
      NULL
    }
  }, .progress = TRUE,
  .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE))

#beep(sound = 8)
plan(sequential)

# 1. Filter out the failed models
good_models <- model_results[!sapply(model_results, is.null)]

# 2. Extract fit indices from each model.
#    We extract AIC, BIC, RMSEA, CFI, TLI, and SRMR.
fit_indices_list <- lapply(good_models, function(x) {
  indices <- lavaan::fitMeasures(x$fit, c("aic", "bic", "rmsea", "cfi", "tli", "srmr"))
  # Combine the model string with its fit indices
  c(model = x$model, indices)
})

# Convert the list to a data frame
fit_indices_df <- do.call(rbind, fit_indices_list) |>
  as.data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Convert numeric columns from character to numeric
numeric_cols <- c("aic", "bic", "rmsea", "cfi", "tli", "srmr")
fit_indices_df[numeric_cols] <- lapply(fit_indices_df[numeric_cols], as.numeric)

# 3. Rank the models by AIC (lowest AIC is considered better)
best_models <- fit_indices_df[order(fit_indices_df$aic), ]

# View the top 10 best models based on AIC
head(best_models, 10)

top_n <-1
# Extract the indices for the top N best models
best_model_indices <- as.numeric(rownames(best_models))[1:top_n]

# Loop through the selected models and plot each
for(i in best_model_indices) {
  semPaths(good_models[[i]]$fit, what = "std", layout = "tree", edge.label.cex = 1.2)
}

mod <- modindices(good_models[[344]]$fit)
```
